Expression: ====== 202604 Batch — Pain / Gout / Neurology / Pharmacy Letters ======

Definition + Context: Extremely intense pain — the most severe level (8–10 on VAS) — equivalent to kidney stone pain
Example Sentence: The patient described the pain as excruciating — he rated it 9 out of 10 and was unable to walk.

Definition + Context: A 0–10 numeric scale used to measure a patient's reported pain intensity
Example Sentence: The pharmacist used the Visual Analogue Scale to assess his pain before recommending analgesia.

Definition + Context: Pain so severe the patient cannot tolerate it — typically 8–10 on VAS
Example Sentence: She described the pain as unbearable — she had not slept for two nights and could not function.

Definition + Context: Significant pain that interferes with daily activities — typically 7–10 on VAS
Example Sentence: He presented with severe pain in his right big toe that had started overnight.

Definition + Context: A pulsating or rhythmic pain — often associated with inflammation infection or vascular conditions
Example Sentence: The gout flare caused a throbbing pain in the joint that was worse at night.

Definition + Context: An extremely intense headache — as if the head is being split apart — severe variant
Example Sentence: He described a splitting headache that had come on suddenly during exertion.

Definition + Context: A diffuse low-level muscular pain — common with viral illness or overexertion
Example Sentence: She reported achy muscles across her whole body — consistent with a viral illness.

Definition + Context: Extremely exhausting or severe — emotionally loaded term — used by patients to describe debilitating conditions
Example Sentence: The fatigue from her condition was brutal — she described it as completely draining her will to function.

Definition + Context: A type of inflammatory arthritis caused by uric acid crystal deposition — typically affects the big toe — excruciating pain
Example Sentence: He was diagnosed with gout after presenting with acute excruciating pain in his right big toe.

Definition + Context: The hallux — most common site of gout — ask specifically about this joint in relevant presentations
Example Sentence: The pain was localised to his big toe and had developed overnight — highly suggestive of gout.

Definition + Context: Colchicine is a gout treatment — the most common side effect is diarrhoea — dose-limiting in many patients
Example Sentence: He was started on colchicine for the acute gout flare but developed significant diarrhoea after the second dose.

Definition + Context: Allopurinol (urate-lowering therapy) can cause a serious skin rash — Stevens-Johnson syndrome in rare cases
Example Sentence: The patient developed a skin rash three weeks after starting allopurinol — the medication was immediately withheld.

Definition + Context: A standard clinical question opener — asking about non-pharmacological treatment attempts
Example Sentence: Apart from medications, did you try any other sort of treatment — heat therapy, rest, elevation?

Definition + Context: British informal — mentally absent or confused due to medication or illness
Example Sentence: The sedative made him feel completely away with the fairies — he could not hold a conversation.

Definition + Context: General terms for impaired mental clarity — often used together to describe drug-induced cognitive effects
Example Sentence: She felt confused and disconnected after starting the new medication — she described it as being lightheaded all day.

Definition + Context: British informal for nausea — does not always mean vomiting
Example Sentence: She called the pharmacy to say she was feeling very sick after taking the first dose.

Expression: The pain didn't get any better — in fact quite the opposite
Definition + Context: Patient expression for worsening symptoms despite treatment — signals treatment failure
Example Sentence: He reported that the pain had not got any better — in fact quite the opposite — it had doubled overnight.

Expression: I was in too much pain to drive
Definition + Context: Functional limitation statement — signals severity of pain and impact on daily activity
Example Sentence: He had been unable to attend his follow-up appointment — he was in too much pain to drive.

Expression: What have you been taking to deal with the pain?
Definition + Context: Standard pain management history question — open phrasing invites full self-medication disclosure
Example Sentence: What have you been taking to deal with the pain? Include any tablets patches or herbal products.

Expression: I can't say they made much difference
Definition + Context: Patient expression for treatment ineffectiveness — mild understatement
Example Sentence: She had tried three different OTC pain relievers but could not say they had made much difference.

Definition + Context: Exhausting and overwhelming — used by patients to describe particularly debilitating experiences
Example Sentence: The weeks of sleeplessness had been brutal — she felt she could not carry on without more support.

Definition + Context: Informal expression for feeling generally unwell — malaise fatigue and discomfort combined
Example Sentence: He said he just felt lousy — no single dominant symptom but a general sense of being run down.

Definition + Context: Informal expression for mild general unwellness — typically cold flu or fatigue
Example Sentence: She phoned in saying she had felt under the weather all week and was not sure if she should take her usual medication.

Definition + Context: Feeling depleted and weakened — often from chronic illness poor sleep or inadequate nutrition
Example Sentence: She had been feeling run down for weeks — fatigue poor appetite and difficulty concentrating.

Definition + Context: Informal expression for profuse sweating — can indicate fever infection or medication reaction
Example Sentence: He described sweating buckets throughout the night — drenching his sheets repeatedly.

Definition + Context: Extremely severe headache — often sudden onset
Example Sentence: She woke with a splitting headache that she rated 10 out of 10 — the worst of her life.

Definition + Context: Unable to tolerate oral intake due to nausea or vomiting
Example Sentence: She called the pharmacy to report she could not keep anything down — every dose was immediately vomited.

Expression: I was throwing up all day yesterday
Definition + Context: Patient description of persistent vomiting — clinically signals dehydration risk and inability to take oral medications
Example Sentence: He reported throwing up all day yesterday — oral medication was not viable and IV access was considered.

Definition + Context: Inflammation of the meninges surrounding the brain — medical emergency — presents with headache neck stiffness photophobia and fever
Example Sentence: The patient presented with a severe headache photophobia and neck stiffness — bacterial meningitis was suspected.

Definition + Context: An antimalarial drug — first-line treatment for severe malaria
Example Sentence: Artesunate was commenced intravenously as the patient had severe falciparum malaria.

Definition + Context: An antimalarial drug associated with serious psychiatric side effects including odd dreams and psychosis
Example Sentence: He had been prescribed mefloquine for malaria prophylaxis and was warned about the risk of odd dreams and psychotic symptoms.

Expression: Some sorts of malaria resistant to these drugs
Definition + Context: Patient phrasing for antimalarial drug resistance — clinically important — affects drug selection
Example Sentence: He was worried because he had heard that some sorts of malaria are resistant to the usual drugs in the region he was visiting.

Definition + Context: Symptoms including delusions hallucinations and disorganised thinking — a serious side effect of mefloquine
Example Sentence: She developed psychotic symptoms during her course of mefloquine — the drug was immediately discontinued.

Definition + Context: Bacterial infection in the bloodstream — a medical emergency — presents with fever hypotension and altered consciousness
Example Sentence: He deteriorated rapidly overnight and developed septicaemia — he was transferred to the ICU immediately.

Definition + Context: Yellow discolouration of skin and sclera — caused by elevated bilirubin — indicates liver or haemolytic disease
Example Sentence: She developed jaundice three days after completing the antibiotic course — liver function tests were ordered.

Definition + Context: A tear in the shoulder rotator cuff muscles — causes pain weakness and restricted movement
Example Sentence: He was diagnosed with a torn rotator cuff following an MRI — surgery was considered after physiotherapy had failed.

Definition + Context: A condition where shoulder tendons are compressed during arm movement — causes pain with overhead activities
Example Sentence: She presented with shoulder impingement syndrome — she had been unable to reach above her head for three months.

Definition + Context: A planned surgical procedure that is not an emergency — the patient can prepare in advance
Example Sentence: The hernia repair was scheduled as elective surgery — he had a limited window to prepare beforehand.

Definition + Context: Unplanned surgery required urgently due to a life-threatening or acutely serious condition
Example Sentence: The bowel obstruction required emergency surgery — there was no time for routine pre-operative preparation.

Definition + Context: A physiotherapy or surgical technique aimed at breaking down adhesions or fibrotic tissue
Example Sentence: The physiotherapist used deep tissue massage to disrupt the scar tissue that had formed after the operation.

Expression: I have a few niggles in my knee
Definition + Context: Patient expression for minor intermittent pain or discomfort — not severe but noticeable
Example Sentence: He said he had a few niggles in his knee when descending stairs but otherwise managed well.

Expression: Just a bit of a niggle
Definition + Context: A minor persistent discomfort that is not severely limiting — British English diminutive expression
Example Sentence: She said it was just a bit of a niggle — not enough to stop her working but enough to notice every day.

Definition + Context: Experienced consequences for overexertion — the pain or fatigue returned after physical activity
Example Sentence: He had pushed through his physiotherapy session and really paid for it — he was unable to walk the next day.

Expression: The patient is alert and responsive
Definition + Context: Baseline neurological status — fully awake and appropriately answering questions
Example Sentence: On assessment the patient was alert and responsive with a GCS of 15.

Definition + Context: A deterioration in level of consciousness — requires urgent assessment
Example Sentence: Over the following hour the patient became less responsive and required immediate medical review.

Definition + Context: Mildly confused or disoriented — not fully alert but still conscious
Example Sentence: He appeared dazed after the fall — he was able to speak but could not recall the event.

Expression: The patient is drowsy but arousable
Definition + Context: Reduced but not absent consciousness — responds to stimulation
Example Sentence: She was drowsy but arousable — she opened her eyes and responded to her name when called.

Definition + Context: Unusually slow and sluggish — lacking normal energy and mental alertness
Example Sentence: He appeared lethargic and was slow to answer questions — consistent with early encephalopathy.

Expression: The patient is disoriented to time and place
Definition + Context: Unable to correctly identify the current time date or location — a sign of cognitive impairment or delirium
Example Sentence: She was disoriented to time and place — she believed she was at home and could not recall the year.

Definition + Context: A worsening deterioration toward unconsciousness — a critical sign
Example Sentence: He became progressively unresponsive over the afternoon — a code was called at 15:30.

Definition + Context: Complete loss of consciousness — no response to verbal or painful stimuli
Example Sentence: By the time the ambulance arrived the patient was unconscious and not breathing normally.

Definition + Context: A subtle low-amplitude tremor — common with lithium or thyroid medication — usually visible in outstretched hands
Example Sentence: It is quite common to notice a fine tremor when taking lithium — please hold out your hands so I can have a look.

Definition + Context: The gap between an effective dose and a toxic dose is very small — requires close monitoring
Example Sentence: Lithium has a very narrow therapeutic window — regular blood tests are essential to keep levels safe.

Definition + Context: A more obvious larger-amplitude tremor — indicates toxicity or severe neurological disturbance
Example Sentence: If the fine tremor changes to a coarse tremor with obvious shaking please contact us immediately.

Definition + Context: Dysarthria — unclear or indistinct speech — a sign of neurological impairment or drug toxicity
Example Sentence: If you notice slurred speech or difficulty forming words seek medical attention urgently.

Definition + Context: Severe drowsiness beyond normal tiredness — a red flag for drug toxicity or neurological emergency
Example Sentence: If you feel profoundly drowsy or cannot stay awake please call 111 immediately.

Definition + Context: A misalignment of the eyes — one or both eyes turn inward outward or upward — also called strabismus or crossed eyes
Example Sentence: She was referred to ophthalmology for assessment of her strabismus — it had been gradually worsening.

Definition + Context: An episode of abnormal electrical activity in the brain — can cause convulsions loss of consciousness or absence
Example Sentence: She was warned that stroboscopic lights at club venues can trigger epileptic seizures.

Definition + Context: A behaviour performed automatically without deliberate thinking — associated with OCD or habitual use
Example Sentence: A compulsive action is something you do automatically without thinking — the patient was unaware he was doing it.

Definition + Context: A non-progressive neurological condition affecting movement and posture — caused by brain injury before or around birth
Example Sentence: Patients with cerebral palsy often require mobility support devices and regular physiotherapy.

Definition + Context: A mood disorder with alternating episodes of mania and depression
Example Sentence: He had been diagnosed with bipolar disorder and was maintained on lithium for mood stabilisation.

Definition + Context: A severe mental illness characterised by hallucinations delusions and disorganised thinking
Example Sentence: She had a history of schizophrenia and was taking clozapine — regular monitoring was required.

Definition + Context: A neurodevelopmental disorder characterised by inattention hyperactivity and impulsivity
Example Sentence: ADHD stands for Attention Deficit Hyperactivity Disorder — it is typically managed with stimulant medication.

Definition + Context: Difficulty breathing or breathlessness — even at rest or with minimal exertion — also called dyspnoea
Example Sentence: She reported shortness of breath even while walking slowly — her oxygen saturation was 91% on room air.

Definition + Context: Pain in the abdomen — location character and radiation should all be assessed
Example Sentence: He presented with severe abdominal pain in the right iliac fossa — appendicitis was the working diagnosis.

Definition + Context: A sensation of fullness or distension in the abdomen — common in IBS and food intolerances
Example Sentence: Her main symptom was bloating after meals — it had been worsening over the past three months.

Definition + Context: An awareness of the heartbeat — racing fluttering or pounding — always in plural form
Example Sentence: She described palpitations that lasted for several minutes — an ECG was ordered to exclude arrhythmia.

Definition + Context: Loss of sensation in a body part — can indicate nerve compression poor circulation or neuropathy
Example Sentence: He reported numbness in his fingers that was worse in the mornings — consistent with carpal tunnel syndrome.

Definition + Context: A temporary loss of consciousness due to reduced blood flow to the brain — may be cardiac vasovagal or orthostatic
Example Sentence: She fainted in the shower — a tilt-table test was arranged to investigate vasovagal syncope.

Definition + Context: Loss of hair from the scalp or body — can be a side effect of chemotherapy or certain medications
Example Sentence: She reported significant hair loss since starting the new medication — the pharmacist documented this as an adverse event.

Definition + Context: A tendency to develop bruises with minimal trauma — may indicate low platelets or anticoagulant therapy
Example Sentence: She noticed she was bruising easily — a full blood count and coagulation screen were requested.

Definition + Context: Trouble moving food or liquid from the mouth to the stomach — requires urgent investigation
Example Sentence: He reported progressive difficulty swallowing solids over two months — an urgent endoscopy was arranged.

Definition + Context: Sweating beyond normal thermoregulation — may be a drug side effect infection or hormonal cause
Example Sentence: She developed excessive sweating within days of starting the new antidepressant.

Definition + Context: Alteration in the frequency consistency or urgency of bowel motions — a red flag symptom requiring investigation
Example Sentence: He reported a change in bowel habits over six weeks — alternating diarrhoea and constipation.

Definition + Context: Reduced desire to eat — clinically significant if unexplained or persistent — may indicate malignancy depression or drug effect
Example Sentence: She described a significant loss of appetite — she had lost four kilograms without trying over the past month.

Definition + Context: Visible enlargement of a joint due to fluid or inflammation — associated with gout arthritis or infection
Example Sentence: The right knee showed marked joint swelling with warmth and tenderness — joint aspiration was performed.

Definition + Context: A squeezing or pressure sensation in the chest — may indicate cardiac respiratory or musculoskeletal cause
Example Sentence: He described chest tightness on exertion — a stress ECG was arranged to rule out cardiac ischaemia.

Definition + Context: Rapid or unpredictable changes in emotional state — may be a drug side effect or indicate a mood disorder
Example Sentence: She reported significant mood swings since starting the new contraceptive — an alternative was discussed.

Definition + Context: Loss of visual clarity — may indicate ocular side effects of medication or systemic disease
Example Sentence: She reported blurred vision that had started after the new medication was commenced — an ophthalmology referral was arranged.

Definition + Context: Increased frequency of passing urine — may indicate urinary tract infection diabetes or medication effect
Example Sentence: He reported frequent urination especially at night — a urine dipstick and blood glucose were checked.

Definition + Context: Sudden sensations of intense body heat — common in menopause or as a side effect of some medications
Example Sentence: She described hot flashes that woke her several times a night — HRT was discussed as an option.

Expression: I noticed a potential clinical issue with this prescription
Definition + Context: A professional and non-accusatory way to raise a prescribing concern with a GP
Example Sentence: I noticed a potential clinical issue with this prescription and wanted to discuss it with you before dispensing.

Expression: The patient's eGFR is reduced which may affect this medication
Definition + Context: Standard pharmacist communication about renal function and drug dosing
Example Sentence: The patient's eGFR is reduced at 28 mL/min which may affect the safety of this medication at the current dose.

Expression: I am writing to express my professional concern regarding
Definition + Context: Formal opening for a pharmacist referral letter — clinical concern about a patient
Example Sentence: I am writing to express my professional concern regarding Mr Henderson's medication adherence and his current clinical status.

Expression: During his visit to the pharmacy today for a repeat prescription
Definition + Context: Context-setting phrase in a referral letter — establishes when the issue was identified
Example Sentence: During his visit to the pharmacy today for a repeat prescription I observed that his blood pressure was elevated at 175/95.

Expression: His blood pressure was persistently elevated
Definition + Context: A clinical observation documented in a referral letter — signals hypertension not at target
Example Sentence: Upon taking his blood pressure I found it was persistently elevated at 165/95 mmHg on two consecutive readings.

Definition + Context: A pharmacist audit phrase — used when reviewing medication collection records to identify non-adherence
Example Sentence: Upon reviewing his dispensing history it appears that he has been collecting his antihypertensive medication inconsistently.

Expression: A formal medication review may be necessary
Definition + Context: A professional recommendation that the prescriber reassess the patient's overall drug therapy
Example Sentence: Given the complexity of his current regimen a formal medication review may be necessary.

Expression: I would recommend a review of his medication regimen to ensure safe use and optimise his treatment
Definition + Context: Standard OET Writing closing recommendation phrase — specific and action-oriented
Example Sentence: I would recommend a review of his medication regimen to ensure safe use and optimise his treatment given his declining renal function.

Expression: Please do not hesitate to contact me if further information is required
Definition + Context: Standard professional closing for a referral or communication letter
Example Sentence: Please do not hesitate to contact me if further information is required — I am available on the number below.

Expression: Her symptoms are suggestive of fluid retention and possible deterioration in renal function
Definition + Context: Clinical interpretation phrase — symptoms point toward a diagnosis without confirming it
Example Sentence: Her ankle oedema and weight gain are suggestive of fluid retention and possible deterioration in renal function.

Expression: I am writing to raise concerns regarding
Definition + Context: Formal letter opener for expressing a clinical concern — less confrontational than 'I am writing to complain'
Example Sentence: I am writing to raise concerns regarding the patient's current medication regimen and recent clinical changes.

Definition + Context: Standard OET Writing phrase — documents that the patient received advice on a specific topic
Example Sentence: He was counselled on the importance of medication adherence and the risks associated with stopping his antihypertensive abruptly.

Expression: The patient reported increasing use of
Definition + Context: Documents self-reported escalation of a substance or medication
Example Sentence: The patient reported increasing use of ibuprofen over the past month — up to six tablets daily.

Expression: I would recommend an urgent review
Definition + Context: A stronger recommendation phrase — appropriate when the clinical situation is time-sensitive
Example Sentence: I would recommend an urgent review to assess for possible methotrexate toxicity given his reported symptoms.

Expression: I observed that his blood pressure was persistently elevated at 165 over 95
Definition + Context: Specific documented clinical observation — includes the numeric reading for precision
Example Sentence: I observed that his blood pressure was persistently elevated at 165 over 95 mmHg on repeat measurement.

Expression: It appears that he has been irregular in collecting his primary antihypertensive medication
Definition + Context: A non-accusatory documentation phrase for non-adherence identified via dispensing records
Example Sentence: It appears that he has been irregular in collecting his primary antihypertensive medication — there have been three gaps of over two weeks in the past six months.

Expression: While I have provided initial counselling
Definition + Context: Acknowledges pharmacist's prior action before recommending further review
Example Sentence: While I have provided initial counselling on the importance of consistency a formal medication review may be necessary.

Expression: A formal medication review may be necessary
Definition + Context: Referral recommendation phrase — appropriate when a pharmacist has reached the limit of their scope
Example Sentence: A formal medication review may be necessary to determine whether the current regimen remains appropriate.

Expression: He admitted to inadvertently skipping doses due to forgetfulness
Definition + Context: Documented patient self-report of unintentional non-adherence
Example Sentence: He admitted to inadvertently skipping doses due to forgetfulness — a once-daily formulation or dosette box was discussed.

Expression: Expressed concerns regarding the adverse effects specifically a persistent dry cough
Definition + Context: Documents the patient's reported side effect in formal language
Example Sentence: She expressed concerns regarding the adverse effects — specifically a persistent dry cough which she believed started after the recent dosage titration.

Expression: Given his comorbidities and the increased risk of cardiovascular events
Definition + Context: Clinical reasoning phrase — connects the patient's background conditions to the clinical decision
Example Sentence: Given his comorbidities and the increased risk of cardiovascular events I am concerned that his current regimen may no longer be optimal.

Expression: Could you please evaluate whether a switch to an ARB might be more appropriate
Definition + Context: A pharmacist's recommendation framed as a request to the prescriber — collaborative tone
Example Sentence: Could you please evaluate whether a switch to an ARB might be more appropriate to mitigate the side effects while maintaining efficacy?

Expression: I believe this intervention would significantly enhance his compliance and overall treatment outcomes
Definition + Context: A strong outcome-focused closing statement in a pharmacist letter
Example Sentence: I believe this intervention would significantly enhance his compliance and overall treatment outcomes given his current difficulties.

Expression: Should her eGFR fall below 30 mL/min a complete cessation of Metformin may be necessary
Definition + Context: Conditional clinical statement — documents a threshold for medication change
Example Sentence: Should her eGFR fall below 30 mL/min/1.73m² a complete cessation of Metformin may be necessary to prevent lactic acidosis.

Expression: Amiodarone is prescribed to manage atrial fibrillation
Definition + Context: Standard counselling opening — names the drug and its indication
Example Sentence: Amiodarone is prescribed to manage atrial fibrillation — it is a very effective but complex medication with several important side effects.

Expression: Patient is starting a loading dose to build up levels in the body
Definition + Context: Explains the purpose of a higher initial dose — important for patient understanding and adherence
Example Sentence: You are starting a loading dose to build up the drug levels in your body — the dose will be reduced after a few weeks.

Expression: Patient must use high-factor sunscreen even on cloudy days
Definition + Context: Amiodarone counselling point — causes photosensitivity — essential safety advice
Example Sentence: You must use high-factor sunscreen every day even on cloudy days — amiodarone makes your skin very sensitive to UV light.

Expression: Development of a blue-grey skin discolouration
Definition + Context: A known and permanent side effect of long-term amiodarone — must be disclosed upfront
Example Sentence: I need to let you know that long-term amiodarone can cause a blue-grey skin discolouration — this is not dangerous but is permanent.

Expression: You may notice halos around lights at night
Definition + Context: An ocular side effect of amiodarone — common and usually mild
Example Sentence: You may notice halos around lights at night — this is a recognised effect of amiodarone and is usually not a reason to stop.

Expression: Report any yellowing of the skin or eyes
Definition + Context: Instruction to report signs of jaundice — indicates hepatotoxicity — a serious amiodarone side effect
Example Sentence: Report any yellowing of the skin or eyes immediately — this could indicate that the drug is affecting your liver.

Expression: Strictly avoid grapefruit juice as it increases drug concentration
Definition + Context: Standard counselling for drugs metabolised by CYP3A4 — grapefruit is a CYP3A4 inhibitor
Example Sentence: Strictly avoid grapefruit juice as it increases drug concentration and can lead to toxic effects.

Expression: Requires baseline and periodic blood tests
Definition + Context: Monitoring requirement phrase — applies to many high-risk medications
Example Sentence: This medication requires baseline and periodic blood tests — we will check your thyroid liver and lung function regularly.

Expression: Staying upright is vital to prevent painful ulcers
Definition + Context: Alendronate counselling — must remain upright for 30 minutes after taking to prevent oesophageal erosion
Example Sentence: Staying upright after taking this tablet is vital to prevent painful ulcers forming in your oesophagus.

Expression: Swallowing pain or heartburn — report immediately
Definition + Context: Alendronate safety advice — signs of oesophageal irritation require urgent review
Example Sentence: Report immediately if you develop swallowing pain or heartburn after taking the tablet — these can be signs of oesophageal damage.

Expression: Writing the exact frequency is vital
Definition + Context: Documentation principle — the exact dosing frequency must be clearly written in any clinical letter or note
Example Sentence: When documenting the prescription writing the exact frequency is vital — once daily versus twice daily can have significant clinical implications.

Expression: The anti-inflammatory medication you are taking may reduce blood flow to your kidneys
Definition + Context: Patient-friendly explanation of NSAID nephrotoxicity
Example Sentence: The anti-inflammatory medication you are taking may reduce blood flow to your kidneys — this is why we recommend using it only when necessary.

Expression: When this medicine is taken together with your blood pressure medication and diuretics it may further impair kidney function
Definition + Context: Triple whammy effect counselling — NSAIDs plus ACE inhibitor plus diuretic
Example Sentence: When this medicine is taken together with your blood pressure medication and diuretics it may further impair kidney function — this combination should generally be avoided.

Expression: Reduced kidney function may also affect how your diabetes medication is processed in your body
Definition + Context: Explains the clinical impact of renal impairment on metformin clearance
Example Sentence: Reduced kidney function may also affect how your diabetes medication is processed in your body — specifically the risk of metformin accumulation increases.

Expression: It is advisable to limit the use of anti-inflammatory medicines and consider safer alternatives such as paracetamol
Definition + Context: Standard NSAID counselling recommendation for patients with renal impairment
Example Sentence: It is advisable to limit the use of anti-inflammatory medicines and consider safer alternatives such as paracetamol where clinically appropriate.

Expression: If you become unwell especially with vomiting diarrhoea or reduced fluid intake you should temporarily stop certain medications and seek medical advice
Definition + Context: Sick day rules counselling — important for patients on metformin ACE inhibitors diuretics or NSAIDs
Example Sentence: If you become unwell — especially with vomiting diarrhoea or reduced fluid intake — you should temporarily stop certain medications and seek medical advice.

Expression: Please continue taking your medication as advised
Definition + Context: Standard adherence instruction — closing line in counselling
Example Sentence: Please continue taking your medication as advised — do not stop without first speaking to your pharmacist or doctor.

Expression: Please ensure that the medication is administered as prescribed
Definition + Context: Formal instruction to a carer or care home — documents the expected administration practice
Example Sentence: Please ensure that the medication is administered as prescribed — any deviation should be reported to the GP.

Expression: I would recommend a review of the patient's medication regimen
Definition + Context: A professional recommendation to a prescriber — invites clinical collaboration
Example Sentence: I would recommend a review of the patient's medication regimen in light of his recent blood test results.

Expression: It's quite common to notice a fine tremor
Definition + Context: Reassurance phrase — normalises a benign drug side effect
Example Sentence: It is quite common to notice a fine tremor when taking this medication — please show me your hands so I can assess it.

Expression: Lithium has a very narrow therapeutic window
Definition + Context: Core lithium counselling fact — explains why monitoring is essential
Example Sentence: Lithium has a very narrow therapeutic window — the difference between a helpful dose and a toxic one is very small.

Expression: If that changes to a coarse tremor where the shaking becomes much more obvious or jerky
Definition + Context: Lithium toxicity warning — escalation sign that requires urgent review
Example Sentence: If the fine tremor changes to a coarse tremor where the shaking becomes much more obvious or jerky please contact us immediately.

Expression: If you start to have slurred speech feel profoundly drowsy or experience severe vomiting
Definition + Context: Lithium toxicity red flag symptoms — requires immediate medical attention
Example Sentence: If you start to have slurred speech feel profoundly drowsy or experience severe vomiting seek emergency medical care immediately.

Expression: Diagnosis was confirmed by a bone density scan
Definition + Context: Standard clinical documentation phrase — names the investigation that confirmed the diagnosis
Example Sentence: The diagnosis of osteoporosis was confirmed by a bone density scan showing a T-score of minus 2.8.

Expression: Not supposed to lie down for at least 30 minutes after administration
Definition + Context: Alendronate dosing instruction — prevents oesophageal irritation
Example Sentence: You are not supposed to lie down for at least 30 minutes after taking this tablet — it must be swallowed with a full glass of water while sitting upright.

Expression: Mrs Rossi must remain upright for at least 30 minutes following the administration of Alendronate to prevent esophageal irritation
Definition + Context: Formal instruction in an OET care home letter — specific patient name drug and rationale included
Example Sentence: Regarding specific precautions Mrs Rossi must remain upright for at least 30 minutes following the administration of Alendronate to prevent oesophageal irritation.

Definition + Context: No symptoms are present — the condition exists but is not yet detectable through symptoms alone
Example Sentence: She was asymptomatic at the time of diagnosis — the condition was detected incidentally on a blood test.

Definition + Context: So small as to be unimportant — does not require clinical attention
Example Sentence: The interaction between these two drugs is negligible at standard doses and does not require any action.

Definition + Context: A very small amount of change or pain — present but barely noticeable
Example Sentence: She reported minimal discomfort at the injection site — no treatment was required.

Definition + Context: Present and noticeable but not significantly limiting daily activities
Example Sentence: He had mild symptoms that did not prevent him from working — watchful waiting was recommended.

Definition + Context: Between mild and severe — requires management but not emergency intervention
Example Sentence: Her condition was classified as moderate — pharmacological treatment was initiated.

Definition + Context: Beyond moderate — approaching severe — may require dose change or escalation of treatment
Example Sentence: The pain was described as moderately severe — the current analgesia was no longer sufficient.

Definition + Context: Significantly affecting function — requires active treatment — may require hospitalisation
Example Sentence: She presented with severe symptoms that had prevented her from leaving the house for three days.

Definition + Context: Life-threatening or immediately dangerous — requires emergency management
Example Sentence: He was in a critical condition on arrival — he was taken directly to the resuscitation bay.

Definition + Context: A complete or near-complete loss of a specific function — often used with hearing or cognition
Example Sentence: She had a profound hearing loss in her right ear following the infection — cochlear implant was discussed.

Expression: What have you been taking to deal with the pain
Definition + Context: Open clinical question for self-medication history — non-judgemental phrasing
Example Sentence: What have you been taking to deal with the pain? That helps me make sure nothing interacts with what I'd like to recommend.

Expression: Did you try any other sort of treatment apart from the medications
Definition + Context: Open question about non-pharmacological self-management
Example Sentence: Did you try any other sort of treatment apart from the medications — such as heat packs rest or physiotherapy?

Expression: The pharmacist responds with heavy medical jargon and the patient just zones out
Definition + Context: A teaching point — using complex language loses patient engagement
Example Sentence: If the pharmacist responds with heavy medical jargon the patient just zones out — always aim for plain language.

Expression: It has the exact same active ingredient as your usual brand — it is just made by a different company and costs less
Definition + Context: Generic substitution explanation — reassures patient about equivalence
Example Sentence: It has the exact same active ingredient as your usual brand — it is just made by a different company and costs less. The effect will be identical.

Expression: If you have been under the weather this medication will help you recover
Definition + Context: Empathetic counselling opener using patient language
Example Sentence: If you have been under the weather lately this medication will help your body recover more quickly.

Expression: Acknowledging the patient's anxiety will instantly build rapport
Definition + Context: A communication principle — validated empathy improves the consultation quality
Example Sentence: Acknowledging the patient's anxiety will instantly build rapport — start by saying 'I can see this has been worrying for you.'

Definition + Context: Standard OET writing phrase — documents how the patient came to the pharmacist
Example Sentence: He presented to my pharmacy today requesting a repeat supply of his regular medications.

Expression: Sought an over-the-counter remedy for a cough
Definition + Context: Documents the patient's self-referral reason in a clinical letter
Example Sentence: She sought an over-the-counter remedy for a cough — during the consultation I identified a potential drug interaction.

Expression: Developed a dry tickly cough that worsens at night and causes insomnia
Definition + Context: Detailed symptom description — includes character timing and functional impact
Example Sentence: He developed a dry tickly cough that worsens at night and causes insomnia — this is consistent with an ACE inhibitor cough.

Expression: I noticed a potential clinical issue with this prescription
Definition + Context: Professional non-threatening opener when calling a prescriber about a concern
Example Sentence: I noticed a potential clinical issue with this prescription — I wanted to run it by you before dispensing.

Expression: Presented to my pharmacy today for a repeat prescription
Definition + Context: Standard OET clinical opening — establishes context for the encounter
Example Sentence: She presented to my pharmacy today for a repeat prescription of her antihypertensive medication.

Definition + Context: Complete avoidance of alcohol — stronger than moderation or reduction
Example Sentence: Complete alcohol abstinence is essential during metronidazole — even small amounts cause flushing and nausea.

Definition + Context: Iron supplement prescribed for iron deficiency anaemia
Example Sentence: Ferrous sulfate is best absorbed on an empty stomach but can be taken with food if GI side effects occur.

Definition + Context: The process by which a drug or nutrient passes from the gut into the bloodstream
Example Sentence: Taking ferrous sulfate with food may slightly reduce absorption but is preferable to stopping altogether.

Definition + Context: A prescribed duration of treatment — must be completed even if symptoms improve
Example Sentence: It is important to complete the full course of ferrous sulfate even if you start feeling better.

Definition + Context: Darkening of bowel motions — common harmless side effect of ferrous sulfate
Example Sentence: Your stools may appear darker than usual — this is nothing to be concerned about and is simply the iron passing through.

Expression: it is nothing to be concerned about
Definition + Context: Reassurance phrase for harmless side effects
Example Sentence: The dark colour is nothing to be concerned about — it is a well-known and harmless effect of this medication.

Expression: even if you start feeling better
Definition + Context: Pre-empts premature discontinuation — patient must continue despite symptom improvement
Example Sentence: Even if you start feeling better please continue taking the full course — your iron stores need more time to recover.

Expression: this may slightly reduce absorption but it is better than stopping altogether
Definition + Context: Balancing phrase — acknowledges a compromise while recommending continuation
Example Sentence: Taking it with food may slightly reduce absorption but it is better than stopping altogether if the side effects are too uncomfortable.

Definition + Context: Cardiac glycoside — narrow therapeutic index — used for heart failure and atrial fibrillation
Example Sentence: Digoxin has a narrow therapeutic index — even small changes in dose can lead to toxicity.

Definition + Context: Forward-looking time marker — signals a new ongoing instruction
Example Sentence: From now on please inform your pharmacist about all supplements you take including herbal products.

Expression: even if it is herbal or from a health food shop
Definition + Context: Emphasises that natural products can still interact with prescription medicines
Example Sentence: Please tell your pharmacist about everything you take — even if it is herbal or from a health food shop.

Definition + Context: SSRI antidepressant — takes 2 to 4 weeks to show full effect — do not stop suddenly
Example Sentence: Sertraline may take two to four weeks to have its full effect — do not stop taking it suddenly without medical advice.

Definition + Context: Potentially life-threatening reaction from excess serotonin activity — caused by combining serotonergic drugs
Example Sentence: Combining sertraline with St John's Wort significantly increases the risk of serotonin syndrome.

Definition + Context: Herbal supplement with antidepressant and enzyme-inducing properties — interacts with many prescription drugs
Example Sentence: St John's Wort is available over the counter but can seriously affect how prescription medications work.

Expression: I can see you are struggling with your mood
Definition + Context: Empathy opener — validates emotional state without diagnosing — invites further discussion
Example Sentence: I can see you are struggling with your mood — would it be alright if I asked a few more questions about how you have been feeling?

Expression: anaphylaxis can happen unexpectedly so being prepared is life-saving
Definition + Context: Core anaphylaxis counselling statement — emphasises unpredictability and importance of carrying auto-injector
Example Sentence: Anaphylaxis can happen unexpectedly so being prepared is life-saving — always carry your adrenaline auto-injector.

Expression: when eating out always tell the restaurant or chef about your severe peanut allergy
Definition + Context: Standard allergy counselling — do not assume staff will remember
Example Sentence: When eating out always tell the restaurant or chef about your severe peanut allergy — do not assume they will remember.

Definition + Context: To combine or simplify into one — in pharmacy: organise medications into a simpler regimen — in respiratory: alveoli filled with fluid (e.g. pneumonia)
Example Sentence: The pharmacist suggested consolidating the morning medications into a single dose time to improve adherence.

Definition + Context: To use or give together — do not combine X with Y = do not take at the same time
Example Sentence: It is essential that you do not combine St John's Wort with sertraline.

Definition + Context: Informal term for vital signs — a group of measurements: BP / HR / RR / Temp / SpO2
Example Sentence: The nurse reported that the patient's vital stats are stable — BP 118 over 76 heart rate 74 sats 98 percent.

Definition + Context: Drug that relieves nasal congestion — must not be used more than 3 to 5 days due to rebound risk
Example Sentence: The pharmacist advised against using the decongestant for more than three consecutive days.

Definition + Context: Worsening of congestion after stopping or overusing a nasal decongestant — also called rhinitis medicamentosa
Example Sentence: Prolonged use of nasal decongestants can lead to rebound congestion — the congestion returns worse than before.

Definition + Context: Sodium chloride solution — used as nasal spray or IV fluid — safer long-term alternative to decongestants
Example Sentence: A saline nasal spray can help relieve congestion without the risk of rebound effects.

Expression: it may not work as quickly as the decongestant
Definition + Context: Expectation-management phrase — used when recommending a safer but slower alternative
Example Sentence: It may not work as quickly as the decongestant but it is much safer for long-term use.

Expression: it should help you feel better
Definition + Context: Closing reassurance phrase — modal should = confident expectation without over-promising
Example Sentence: Keep using it regularly — it should help you feel better within a few days.

Definition + Context: Vitamin K antagonist anticoagulant — narrow therapeutic index — many drug and food interactions
Example Sentence: Patients taking warfarin must be counselled carefully about interactions — including decongestants and NSAIDs.

Expression: X-ray machine at airport security may alarm if carrying insulin supplies
Definition + Context: Travel counselling for diabetic patients — metal in devices may trigger security alarms — carry a medical letter
Example Sentence: You should be aware that the X-ray machine at airport security may alarm when carrying insulin supplies — I recommend getting a letter from your GP.

Definition + Context: Reduced effectiveness of a drug — bacteria or the body no longer responding — or drug interaction reducing plasma levels
Example Sentence: Rifampicin can reduce the effectiveness of oral contraceptives through enzyme induction — not true resistance but a similar effect.

Definition + Context: A drug or device that prevents pregnancy — oral contraceptive effectiveness reduced by enzyme inducers
Example Sentence: Rifampicin significantly reduces the effectiveness of the contraceptive pill — additional contraception is required.

Definition + Context: Potent hepatic enzyme inducer used in TB treatment — reduces plasma levels of many co-administered drugs
Example Sentence: Rifampicin interacts with warfarin oral contraceptives digoxin and many other medications.

Definition + Context: Infectious disease caused by Mycobacterium tuberculosis — treated with combination antibiotic therapy for at least 6 months
Example Sentence: Tuberculosis is treated with a combination of rifampicin isoniazid pyrazinamide and ethambutol.

Definition + Context: Standard phrase for drug interaction where one drug lowers the therapeutic effect of another
Example Sentence: Rifampicin reduces the effectiveness of the oral contraceptive pill by inducing liver enzymes that speed up its metabolism.

Definition + Context: The process of injecting insulin — includes technique site rotation timing and storage
Example Sentence: Correct insulin administration technique including site rotation is essential to prevent lipodystrophy.

Definition + Context: All items needed for insulin management — pens needles syringes glucose monitor lancets strips
Example Sentence: When travelling always carry insulin supplies in hand luggage along with a medical letter.

Definition + Context: Vitamin or dietary addition — not a prescription medicine — can still cause drug interactions
Example Sentence: When a patient says they take supplements always clarify — some herbal products like St John's Wort interact with prescription medicines.

Definition + Context: To arrange a booking for an endoscopic procedure — clinical recommendation language
Example Sentence: Given your symptoms I would like to schedule an endoscopy to examine the lining of your stomach directly.

Definition + Context: Patient-friendly term for the gastric mucosa — the inner surface of the stomach
Example Sentence: The endoscopy allows us to look directly at the lining of your stomach and check for inflammation or ulcers.

Definition + Context: Informal patient language for abdominal pain — especially used by or with children
Example Sentence: She described the tummy pain as a burning sensation that starts about twenty minutes after eating.

Definition + Context: Patient language for nerve compression or entrapment — may cause radiating pain or numbness
Example Sentence: She was concerned the shooting pain in her arm might be a pinched nerve in her neck.

Definition + Context: An electric heated pad used for symptom relief — British equivalent of hot water bottle
Example Sentence: She had been using a heating pad on her lower back but the relief was only temporary.

Definition + Context: Patient-friendly explanation of the magnetic field in an MRI scanner — relevant for patients with metal implants
Example Sentence: Patients with certain implants cannot have an MRI because of the magnetism from the machine.

Expression: I have gone through your notes
Definition + Context: Clinical consultation opener — signals the clinician has reviewed the records and is prepared
Example Sentence: I have gone through your notes and I can see you have been experiencing these symptoms for some time.

Expression: the last sort of month it has been more and more
Definition + Context: Patient language for escalating symptoms over approximately one month — hedge word sort of signals approximation
Example Sentence: The last sort of month it has been more and more — I can barely get through a meal without the pain starting.

Expression: the worst has been in the last month
Definition + Context: Patient self-assessment of symptom peak — superlative signals clinical significance
Example Sentence: Long before that the last six months were tough but the worst has been in the last month.

Expression: I have been trying bits and pieces
Definition + Context: Patient has self-managed with various remedies without a systematic approach — signals prior self-medication
Example Sentence: I have been trying bits and pieces — antacids cutting out coffee — but nothing works long-term.

Expression: particularly when you have food I suppose
Definition + Context: Clinician checking a possible trigger — hedged question using I suppose to soften the assumption
Example Sentence: Given that you have this problem particularly when you have food I suppose — does the pain tend to start after meals?

Definition + Context: Self-correction marker — the figure that follows is the accurate one — overrides the estimate before it
Example Sentence: The pain starts about twenty minutes after eating — thirty minutes to be precise.

Expression: I can still get the symptoms
Definition + Context: Patient reports symptoms persist despite treatment or avoidance — signals incomplete resolution
Example Sentence: Even when I follow all the dietary advice I can still get the symptoms — usually late at night.

Expression: I have swallowed a knife and it is stabbing inside my tummy
Definition + Context: Patient metaphor for sharp stabbing epigastric pain — decode as: sharp pain
Example Sentence: It feels like I have swallowed a knife and it is stabbing inside my tummy — that is the only way I can describe it.

Expression: something is on fire inside my tummy
Definition + Context: Patient metaphor for burning epigastric pain — decode as: pyrosis / burning sensation
Example Sentence: There is something on fire inside my tummy — a burning feeling that goes all the way up to my chest.

Expression: Can I know a little bit more about your diet
Definition + Context: Soft open question about dietary habits — less direct than asking about diet
Example Sentence: Can I know a little bit more about your diet? Sometimes certain foods can trigger these kinds of symptoms.

Definition + Context: Patient has not identified a specific trigger — signals further investigation or diary keeping is needed
Example Sentence: I have been keeping a food diary for two weeks but I haven't pinpointed anything yet.

Expression: it is a tube passed through your throat with a camera at the end
Definition + Context: Plain English explanation of an endoscopy — essential OET patient-explanation language
Example Sentence: An endoscopy — it is a tube passed through your throat with a camera at the end — lets us look directly at the stomach lining.

Definition + Context: Musculoskeletal pain with a clear mechanical trigger — lifting as causative activity
Example Sentence: He described it as just pain related to lifting — it only comes on when he carries heavy objects.

Definition + Context: Clinical closing phrase — signals the decision is not final and the patient will be reviewed
Example Sentence: Start the medication and come back in two weeks — we will reassess at that point and adjust if needed.

Definition + Context: 1. Neurological symptoms without organic cause (conversion disorder) 2. Changing a drug from IV to oral
Example Sentence: After all organic causes were excluded the team considered a diagnosis of conversion disorder.

Definition + Context: Involuntary rhythmic muscle contractions — a seizure episode
Example Sentence: The child experienced a febrile convulsion during a high fever which resolved spontaneously within two minutes.

Definition + Context: Medical term for persistent bad breath — causes include poor oral hygiene gum disease GORD dry mouth
Example Sentence: The patient presented with persistent halitosis that had not improved despite regular brushing.

Definition + Context: The root or primary cause beneath the surface symptom — always treat the underlying cause not just the symptom
Example Sentence: The mouthwash may help short-term but we need to identify and treat the underlying cause.

Definition + Context: Practices that maintain the health of the mouth — brushing flossing mouthwash regular dental check-ups
Example Sentence: Poor oral hygiene is the most common cause of halitosis and can usually be improved with consistent brushing and flossing.

Definition + Context: Having properties that kill or inhibit bacterial growth — can be topical (mouthwash cream) or systemic (antibiotic)
Example Sentence: An antibacterial mouthwash containing chlorhexidine may help reduce the bacterial load responsible for halitosis.

Definition + Context: To book or arrange an endoscopic procedure — standard clinical recommendation
Example Sentence: I would like to schedule an endoscopy for you — we need to look at the lining of your stomach directly.

Definition + Context: Sodium chloride solution used as a nasal rinse — safer long-term alternative to decongestants
Example Sentence: A saline nasal spray can help moisturise the nasal passages without the risk of rebound congestion.

Definition + Context: Worsening of nasal congestion after overusing or stopping a nasal decongestant
Example Sentence: Using a decongestant for more than five days can cause rebound congestion — the nose becomes more blocked than before.

Definition + Context: A specialist doctor who diagnoses and treats conditions of the digestive system
Example Sentence: He was referred to a gastroenterologist after his GP suspected irritable bowel syndrome.

Definition + Context: A common functional disorder of the digestive system causing bloating unpredictable bowel habits and abdominal pain
Example Sentence: Irritable bowel syndrome is not caused by structural damage — it is a functional condition affecting how the gut moves.

Definition + Context: An over-the-counter medication that neutralises stomach acid — used for heartburn and indigestion
Example Sentence: She had been managing her heartburn with antacids from the pharmacy for several years.

Definition + Context: A feeling of fullness or distension in the abdomen — common in IBS food intolerances and bowel conditions
Example Sentence: The patient's main complaint was bloating after meals which had been worsening over the past year.

Definition + Context: Infrequent or difficult passage of stools — fewer than three bowel motions per week
Example Sentence: He had been suffering from constipation for the past month which was unusual for him.

Definition + Context: A severe recurring headache — often one-sided — with nausea and light sensitivity — British RP: MEE-grayn
Example Sentence: She experienced migraines at the same time as her stomach symptoms — she had not initially connected the two.

Definition + Context: Persistent difficulty falling or staying asleep — can be onset (can't get to sleep) or maintenance (waking in the night)
Example Sentence: He went to bed exhausted every night but suffered from insomnia — much more than he had ever experienced before.

Definition + Context: A professional who advises on diet and nutrition — not the same as a dietitian in all contexts
Example Sentence: A nutritionist had advised him to increase his fluid intake and review his fibre consumption.

Definition + Context: Indigestible plant material that supports bowel health — found in vegetables wholegrains and legumes
Example Sentence: The nutritionist suggested his fibre intake might be insufficient though the patient disagreed.

Definition + Context: Foods made from animal milk — butter cheese yoghurt milk — can worsen IBS in some patients
Example Sentence: He tried cutting out dairy products to see if they were contributing to his symptoms but noticed no improvement.

Definition + Context: A drug that relaxes smooth muscle in the gut — used to relieve cramping in IBS — e.g. mebeverine
Example Sentence: His GP prescribed an antispasmodic to relax the muscular contractions that move food through the gut.

Definition + Context: The rhythmic squeezing movements of the gut wall that move food through the digestive tract
Example Sentence: Antispasmodics work by relaxing the muscular contractions responsible for cramping pain.

Definition + Context: Medications that target nerve signals — in IBS context used for their effect on the gut-brain axis not for depression per se
Example Sentence: He had been given antidepressants and was giving them a go — though he was not entirely sure why.

Definition + Context: A specialist doctor who diagnoses and treats conditions of the nervous system
Example Sentence: She was referred to a neurologist after physiotherapy failed to resolve her symptoms.

Definition + Context: A healthcare practitioner who treats musculoskeletal problems through manipulation
Example Sentence: She visited an osteopath hoping for relief from her neck stiffness but the treatment made things worse.

Definition + Context: A neurological condition causing involuntary pulling or twisting of the head to one side — also called cervical dystonia
Example Sentence: She was diagnosed with spasmodic torticollis — a condition causing her head to pull involuntarily towards her right shoulder.

Definition + Context: Manual therapy focused on the spine and musculoskeletal system — provided by a chiropractor
Example Sentence: She tried chiropractic treatment for six months at great expense but it provided no lasting benefit.

Definition + Context: A group of people with a shared condition who meet to provide mutual understanding and information
Example Sentence: Joining a support group for spasmodic torticollis patients gave her a sense of community.

Definition + Context: A chair designed to support correct posture and reduce musculoskeletal strain
Example Sentence: She purchased an orthopaedic chair at home to provide postural support and reduce her pain levels.

Definition + Context: Botulinum toxin injected into muscles to treat dystonia spasm or chronic migraine — not cosmetic in this context
Example Sentence: She read about Botox injections being used at a university hospital to treat spasmodic torticollis.

Definition + Context: Difficulty or discomfort when swallowing — can be a side effect of Botox injections in the neck
Example Sentence: A known side effect of Botox injections in the neck region is temporary problems swallowing.

Definition + Context: To increase the dose — informal phrasing — clinical equivalent: uptitrate or increase the dose
Example Sentence: Even after upping the dosage the subsequent injections were not as effective as the first round.

Definition + Context: A patient-controlled analgesia device allowing self-administration of morphine for pain relief
Example Sentence: For pain management she was eventually given a pump so she could self-administer morphine as needed.

Definition + Context: A condition where uterine tissue grows outside the uterus — causing pelvic pain bloating and fatigue
Example Sentence: She had been living with endometriosis for many years before it was formally diagnosed.

Definition + Context: Intermittent episodes of nausea — not constant but recurring
Example Sentence: She experienced occasional bouts of nausea that came on without warning after meals.

Definition + Context: Cancer of the cervix — the lower part of the uterus
Example Sentence: She was eventually diagnosed with cervical cancer which fortunately had not advanced too far.

Definition + Context: Surgical removal of the uterus — may also include ovaries and fallopian tubes
Example Sentence: She was advised to have a hysterectomy and the procedure went relatively smoothly.

Definition + Context: Low red blood cell count due to insufficient iron — causes fatigue pallor and breathlessness
Example Sentence: The doctor initially suspected iron deficiency anaemia based on her symptoms of fatigue.

Definition + Context: Excess iron accumulation in the body — as in haemochromatosis
Example Sentence: The blood test results were unexpected — she had iron overload rather than iron deficiency.

Definition + Context: A hereditary condition causing the body to absorb too much iron — treated with regular venesection
Example Sentence: Haemochromatosis is a genetic condition — the doctor asked about family history.

Definition + Context: The therapeutic removal of blood to reduce iron stores — primary treatment for haemochromatosis
Example Sentence: She was told that venesection — regular removal of blood — was the only treatment option.

Definition + Context: Reduced ease of movement in joints — particularly noticeable in the morning or after rest
Example Sentence: She had started to notice stiffness in her joints — particularly in her fingers and knees.

Definition + Context: Difficulty breathing or breathlessness — even at rest or during mild activity
Example Sentence: She occasionally felt short of breath even during light activities like walking the dog.

Definition + Context: Became infected with the COVID-19 virus
Example Sentence: It all started when he caught COVID-19 — he had not been vaccinated at the time.

Definition + Context: Initial symptoms resembling influenza — fever body aches fatigue and cough
Example Sentence: He initially presented with typical flu-like symptoms that later developed into a persistent dry cough.

Definition + Context: Gradually drained or depleted energy levels over time — more gradual than wipe out
Example Sentence: Looking back the whole experience had really sapped his energy — he just did not feel like himself.

Expression: getting out of breath just doing ordinary things
Definition + Context: Breathlessness with minimal exertion — a significant functional limitation
Example Sentence: He was getting out of breath just doing ordinary things like carrying groceries up the stairs.

Expression: brain would go kind of fuzzy
Definition + Context: Informal description of cognitive impairment — brain fog — difficulty thinking clearly
Example Sentence: His brain would go kind of fuzzy and he could not focus on anything properly.

Expression: words would be on the tip of my tongue
Definition + Context: Word-finding difficulty — almost remembering a word but being unable to retrieve it — a specific cognitive symptom
Example Sentence: Words would be on the tip of my tongue but just would not come out — it was very frustrating.

Definition + Context: An awareness of the heartbeat — often described as racing pounding or fluttering — always used in plural
Example Sentence: When he started experiencing palpitations and chest pains he went to see his doctor.

Definition + Context: A recording of the electrical activity of the heart — used to detect arrhythmias and cardiac abnormalities
Example Sentence: He underwent several cardiac tests including an ECG and was placed on a heart monitor for five days.

Definition + Context: An abnormal heart rhythm — can be too fast too slow or irregular
Example Sentence: The heart monitor identified arrhythmia as the cause of his palpitations and chest discomfort.

Definition + Context: Medications that slow the heart rate and reduce blood pressure — used for arrhythmias angina and hypertension
Example Sentence: He was prescribed beta blockers for the arrhythmia and noticed improvement almost immediately.

Definition + Context: Blood test finding — vitamin D deficiency — common in people with limited sun exposure
Example Sentence: Blood tests also revealed that his vitamin D levels were low and supplements were recommended.

Definition + Context: A persistent low-level pain — not sharp or stabbing — often described in joints or muscles
Example Sentence: The joint pain was best described as a dull ache rather than a sharp or stabbing sensation.

Definition + Context: Loss of sensation in the fingers — can indicate nerve compression poor circulation or neuropathy
Example Sentence: He reported a certain amount of numbness in his fingers which he found particularly worrying.

Definition + Context: Elastic bands used for strength training — lower intensity than free weights — suitable for rehabilitation
Example Sentence: The physiotherapist suggested starting with resistance bands before progressing to light weights.

Definition + Context: A sore on the lining of the stomach or duodenum — caused by H. pylori or NSAIDs — presents with burning pain
Example Sentence: He had initially thought it was a stomach ulcer which had been troubling him for some time.

Definition + Context: A procedure where a flexible tube with a camera is passed through the mouth to examine the digestive tract
Example Sentence: He had what the doctor called an endoscopy — a tube passed down the throat to look at the insides.

Definition + Context: The removal of a small tissue sample for laboratory analysis — to rule out malignancy or confirm diagnosis
Example Sentence: As part of the endoscopy a biopsy was taken to make sure it was not something more serious.

Definition + Context: A bacterial infection of the stomach lining — a common cause of peptic ulcers — treated with antibiotics
Example Sentence: The endoscopy confirmed Helicobacter pylori — a course of antibiotics cleared it up completely.

Definition + Context: Surgical repair of a hernia — where tissue protrudes through a weak spot in surrounding muscle
Example Sentence: He had a hernia operation shortly after retiring — he suspected it was an occupational injury.

Definition + Context: An injury or health condition caused or worsened by work activities or environment
Example Sentence: Several of his colleagues had the same problem — he believed it was an occupational injury.

Definition + Context: A gradual depletion of physical energy during activity — fatigue setting in
Example Sentence: He would be digging in the garden and suddenly find himself running out of energy.

Definition + Context: British informal — to not want or have an appetite for something — clinical signal for reduced appetite
Example Sentence: He no longer fancied the sort of big meals he used to enjoy — he would feel sick if he had too much.

Definition + Context: To have a hereditary or genetic tendency within a family — common lay expression for familial predisposition
Example Sentence: He was aware that these conditions can run in families — his uncle had had similar problems.

Definition + Context: A highly significant once-in-a-lifetime travel experience — used to explain the urgency of getting medical clearance
Example Sentence: The Antarctica cruise was the trip of a lifetime and he needed to make sure he was well enough to go.

Definition + Context: Enlarged twisted veins visible under the skin — most common in the legs — can cause aching swelling and itching
Example Sentence: She had been struggling with varicose veins that had progressively worsened over the past four years.

Definition + Context: A coagulated mass of blood — in veins: DVT — in lungs: PE — potentially life-threatening
Example Sentence: An ultrasound was performed to check for a blood clot — thankfully that was all clear.

Definition + Context: A procedure where a foaming agent is injected into varicose veins to seal them — done under local anaesthetic
Example Sentence: The doctor arranged foam injections into the varicose veins — a relatively new outpatient procedure.

Definition + Context: An anaesthetic agent that numbs a specific area without causing unconsciousness
Example Sentence: The procedure was performed under local anaesthetic — she did not need to stay overnight.

Definition + Context: Graduated pressure stockings worn to improve circulation and reduce swelling — prescribed after vein procedures or DVT
Example Sentence: She was required to wear compression stockings for two weeks following the procedure.

Definition + Context: A specialist doctor who treats infants children and adolescents
Example Sentence: The paediatrician referred the child to a specialist clinic for further assessment.

Definition + Context: A chronic inflammatory skin condition causing itching redness and dry scaly patches
Example Sentence: His eczema had flared up significantly over the winter months particularly on his arms.

Definition + Context: A water-based emollient cream used to moisturise dry skin — previously used as a wash for eczema patients
Example Sentence: The GP had initially prescribed aqueous cream but it was causing further skin irritation.

Definition + Context: A thick ointment used as a barrier cream to protect dry or irritated skin — also called Vaseline
Example Sentence: Petroleum jelly can be applied as a barrier cream to protect the skin around inflamed areas.

Definition + Context: A condition on the autism spectrum characterised by difficulty with social interaction and rigid routines
Example Sentence: She mentioned she had heard about conditions such as Asperger's and wondered if her son might be affected.

Definition + Context: Solid deposits that form in the gallbladder — can cause upper right abdominal pain especially after fatty meals
Example Sentence: An ultrasound was ordered to rule out gallstones as a possible cause of her upper abdominal pain.

Definition + Context: A burning sensation in the chest caused by stomach acid rising into the oesophagus — also called acid reflux
Example Sentence: She described a burning sensation in her chest after meals consistent with heartburn.

Definition + Context: The pattern and frequency of a person's bowel movements — clinicians always ask about changes
Example Sentence: The GP asked whether she had noticed any changes in her bowel habits over the past few weeks.

Definition + Context: Pain or swelling located in the lower back of the leg — the calf muscle — clinically relevant for DVT
Example Sentence: She reported a dull aching pain in her calf that had developed after a long-haul flight.

Definition + Context: A first-line oral medication for type 2 diabetes — biguanide class — must be taken with meals to reduce GI side effects
Example Sentence: She had recently been started on metformin and was experiencing nausea when taking it before breakfast.

Definition + Context: An unpleasant sensation of wanting to vomit — common side effect of many medications
Example Sentence: The main side effect she reported was nausea occurring approximately thirty minutes after each dose.

Definition + Context: A formulation that releases the drug slowly over time — causes fewer GI side effects than immediate release
Example Sentence: Switching to extended release metformin may reduce the gastrointestinal side effects significantly.

Definition + Context: A structured review of a patient's medications conducted by a pharmacist to check for problems and improve adherence
Example Sentence: She was invited to attend a medicines use review to discuss her new medications and any concerns.

Definition + Context: High blood pressure — a major risk factor for cardiovascular disease — often asymptomatic
Example Sentence: She had been diagnosed with hypertension the previous year and was taking amlodipine.

Definition + Context: A calcium channel blocker used to treat hypertension and angina — common side effects include ankle swelling and flushing
Example Sentence: She was taking amlodipine ten milligrams once daily for her hypertension.

Definition + Context: A statin medication used to lower cholesterol — can cause muscle aches — must not be taken with grapefruit juice
Example Sentence: Her atorvastatin dose had recently been increased from twenty to forty milligrams.

Definition + Context: A non-steroidal anti-inflammatory drug (NSAID) — reduces pain fever and inflammation — can raise blood pressure and worsen renal function
Example Sentence: She had been taking ibuprofen for her headaches without realising it could affect her blood pressure.

Definition + Context: To increase systemic blood pressure — NSAIDs can do this especially in patients already on antihypertensives
Example Sentence: Ibuprofen can raise blood pressure which is particularly concerning in patients already being treated for hypertension.

Definition + Context: A drop in blood pressure on standing — causing dizziness or lightheadedness — common cause of morning dizziness
Example Sentence: The dizziness on standing first thing in the morning was consistent with postural hypotension.

Definition + Context: The amount of sodium chloride consumed through food — reducing salt helps lower blood pressure
Example Sentence: Her GP had advised her to reduce her salt intake as part of managing her hypertension.

Definition + Context: A common analgesic and antipyretic — first-line choice for pain in patients on warfarin or with peptic ulcer disease
Example Sentence: The pharmacist recommended switching from ibuprofen to paracetamol to avoid the blood pressure interaction.

Definition + Context: Easily upset or stressed — tense and anxious by nature — describes a child or person who reacts strongly to change
Example Sentence: His teachers had alluded to the fact that he is highly strung and struggles to cope with changes in routine.

Definition + Context: To feel completely exhausted — British informal — stronger than tired
Example Sentence: She said she just feels shattered by mid-afternoon and can barely get through the rest of her shift.

Definition + Context: Difficulty falling asleep at the start of the night — sleep onset insomnia — distinct from waking in the night
Example Sentence: It is really hard dropping off to sleep some nights even when she is completely exhausted.

Definition + Context: The burden of responsibility caused low mood or emotional exhaustion — phrasal verb get down = to make someone feel depressed
Example Sentence: She explained that the responsibility really got her down — managing everything alone had been overwhelming.

Definition + Context: The pattern and frequency of a person's bowel movements
Example Sentence: The pharmacist asked whether he had noticed any changes in his bowel habits since starting the new medication.

Definition + Context: The initial seizure episode — onset of epilepsy or a new neurological condition
Example Sentence: The patient experienced her first seizure approximately one month ago while at work.

Definition + Context: Sudden or gradual loss of speech production — may indicate stroke TIA or neurological damage
Example Sentence: She lost the ability to speak and was babbling incoherently — consistent with a dominant hemisphere lesion.

Definition + Context: A gradual worsening of cognitive function consciousness or behaviour over time
Example Sentence: The notes describe progressive deterioration of mental status over a three-week period.

Definition + Context: Any change from baseline mental functioning — includes confusion agitation or reduced consciousness
Example Sentence: She presented with intermittent altered mental status — lucid at times then acutely confused.

Definition + Context: Oxygen saturation falling — a critical clinical sign requiring immediate action
Example Sentence: Her O2 sats are dropping rapidly — we need to secure the airway.

Definition + Context: Blood pressure falling rapidly — a sign of haemodynamic instability
Example Sentence: Blood pressure is plummeting — commence vasopressors immediately.

Definition + Context: Chaotic electrical activity in the ventricles — no effective cardiac output — requires defibrillation immediately
Example Sentence: The monitor showed ventricular fibrillation — we charged the defibrillator and delivered a shock.

Definition + Context: Normal cardiac rhythm re-established after arrhythmia or cardiac arrest
